html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自序 - Formal title meaning 'self-introduction' or 'preface,' commonly used in classical Chinese texts to introduce the author or the work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冥祥記 - Title suggesting a collection of stories or accounts related to supernatural phenomena, omens, or divine interventions, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 南朝齊 - Southern Qi Dynasty, a period of political fragmentation and cultural flourishing in southern China (479–502 CE).
  • 王琰 - Author's name, likely a historian or scholar from the Southern Qi period.

Proper Nouns

  • 冥祥記 - Title of the text, rendered as 'Records of the Supernatural' or 'Narratives of the Mysterious and Auspicious.'
  • 南朝齊 - Southern Qi Dynasty, rendered as 'Southern Qi' in English.
  • 王琰 - Author's name, transliterated as 'Wang Yan.'

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 琰稚年在交阯 - Yan was young when he was in Jiaozhi, where '稚年' means 'young age' and '交阯' refers to the ancient Chinese region of Jiaozhi (modern-day northern Vietnam).
  • 彼土有賢法师者，道德僧也 - There was a virtuous monk in that land, a man of morality, where '賢法师' means 'virtuous monk' and '道德僧' means 'monk of virtue.'
  • 见授五戒，以观世音金像一躯，见与供养 - He was given the Five Precepts and a gold statue of Guanyin, which he was entrusted to worship, where '见授' means 'was given' and '供养' means 'worship' or 'venerate.'
  • 形制异今，又非甚古，类元嘉中作 - Its shape is different from today's, and it is not very ancient, likely made during the Yuanjia period, where '形制' means 'shape' or 'form,' and '元嘉' refers to the reign of Emperor Wen of Liu Song (424–453 CE).
  • 镕镌殊工，似有真好 - The casting and engraving are exceptionally fine, as if they are genuine and exquisite, where '镕镌' means 'casting and engraving,' and '殊工' means 'exceptionally fine work.'
  • 琰奉以还都 - Yan respectfully brought it back to the capital, where '奉以' means 'respectfully brought.'
  • 时年在龆龀 - At that time, he was in his childhood, where '龆龀' refers to early childhood.
  • 治改弊庐，无屋安设 - When he renovated his dilapidated house, there was no room to place it, where '弊庐' means 'dilapidated house' and '安设' means 'to place' or 'to set up.'
  • 寄京师南涧寺中 - He temporarily placed it in the Nanjian Temple in the capital, where '寄' means 'temporarily placed' and '京师' means 'capital.'
  • 百姓竞铸钱，亦有盗毁金像以充铸者 - The common people were competing to mint coins, and some even destroyed the gold statue to use the metal for casting, where '竞铸' means 'competing to mint' and '盗毁' means 'destroyed secretly.'
  • 时像在寺，已经数月 - The statue had been in the temple for several months, where '已经' means 'had been' and '数月' means 'several months.'
  • 琰昼寝，梦见立于座隅 - Yan napped during the day and dreamed that the statue stood by his side, where '昼寝' means 'napped during the day' and '座隅' means 'by his side.'
  • 意甚异之 - He found this very strange, where '意甚异之' means 'found it very strange.'
  • 时日已?，即驰迎还 - When the sun had set, he immediately rushed to bring it back, where '时日已?' means 'when the sun had set' and '驰迎' means 'rushed to bring.'
  • 其夕，南涧十余躯像，悉遇盗亡 - That night, over ten statues in the Nanjian Temple were all stolen, where '悉遇盗亡' means 'all were stolen.'
  • 其后久之，像于曛?闲放光 - After a long time, the statue began to emit light in the twilight, where '曛?' means 'twilight' and '放光' means 'emit light.'
  • 显照，三尺许地，金辉秀起，焕然夺目 - The light illuminated about three feet of ground, and the golden radiance was dazzling, where '显照' means 'illuminate,' '金辉秀起' means 'golden radiance rising,' and '焕然夺目' means 'dazzling.'
  • 琰兄弟及仆役同睹者十余人 - Yan, his brothers, and over ten servants witnessed this together, where '同睹' means 'witnessed together.'
  • 于时幼小，不即题记 - At that time, being young, he did not immediately record it, where '不即题记' means 'did not immediately record.'
  • 比加撰录，忘其日月 - When he later compiled a record, he forgot the date and month, where '比加撰录' means 'when he later compiled a record' and '忘其日月' means 'forgot the date and month.'
  • 是宋大明七年秋也 - This was in the autumn of the seventh year of the Daming era of the Liu Song dynasty (466 CE), where '宋大明七年' refers to the seventh year of the Daming era.
  • 至泰始末，琰移居乌衣，周旋僧以此像权寓多宝寺 - By the end of the Taishi era, Yan moved to Wuyi, and the monk entrusted the statue to the Dabao Temple for temporary storage, where '周旋僧' means 'the monk' and '权寓' means 'temporarily stored.'
  • 琰时暂游江都，此僧仍适荆楚 - When Yan briefly traveled to Jiangdu, the monk went to Jingchu, where '暂游' means 'briefly traveled' and '适荆楚' means 'went to Jingchu.'
  • 不知像处，垂将十载 - He did not know the location of the statue for nearly ten years, where '垂将十载' means 'nearly ten years.'
  • 常恐神宝，与因俱绝 - He often feared that the divine treasure would be lost along with the opportunity, where '常恐' means 'often feared' and '与因俱绝' means 'lost along with the opportunity.'
  • 宋升明末，游踬峡表，经过江陵，见此沙门，乃知像所 - At the end of the Shengming era of the Liu Song dynasty, when he traveled through the Xiang River area and passed through Jiangling, he saw this monk and learned the location of the statue, where '游踬峡表' means 'traveled through the Xiang River area' and '乃知像所' means 'learned the location of the statue.'
  • 其年，琰还京师，即造多宝寺访焉 - That year, Yan returned to the capital and immediately visited the Dabao Temple to inquire about it, where '即造' means 'immediately visited.'
  • 寺主爱公，云无此寄像 - The abbot, Aigong, said there was no such statue in the temple, where '寺主' means 'abbot' and '云无此寄像' means 'said there was no such statue.'
  • 琰退虑此僧孟浪，将遂失此像，深以惆怅 - Yan was worried that the monk had acted rashly and that he might lose the statue, deeply lamenting this, where '退虑' means 'was worried' and '深以惆怅' means 'deeply lamenting.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 交阯 - The ancient Chinese name for the region that is now northern Vietnam.
  • 五戒 - The Five Precepts, which are basic ethical guidelines in Buddhism, including abstaining from killing, stealing, sexual misconduct, lying, and intoxicants.
  • 观世音 - Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 元嘉 - The reign title of Emperor Wen of Liu Song (424–453 CE).
  • 大明七年 - The seventh year of the Daming era of the Liu Song dynasty (466 CE).
  • 泰始末 - The end of the Taishi era of the Liu Song dynasty (472–479 CE).
  • 乌衣 - A district in Nanjing, known for its historical significance and association with the elite.
  • 多宝寺 - The Dabao Temple, a Buddhist temple in Nanjing.
  • 江都 - An ancient city, now part of Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 荆楚 - An ancient region in central and western China, roughly corresponding to modern-day Hubei and Hunan provinces.
  • 升明 - The reign title of Emperor Houzhu of Liu Song (477–479 CE).
  • 峡表 - The area around the Xiang River, which flows through Hunan Province.
  • 江陵 - An ancient city, now part of Jingzhou, Hubei Province.
  • 沙门 - A term for Buddhist monks.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 琰稚年在交阯 - This phrase introduces the setting and the young age of Yan.
  • 彼土有贤法师者，道德僧也 - This clause introduces the virtuous monk and his moral qualities.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 的的分明 - Emphasizes clarity; means 'clearly and distinctly.'
  • 循复其事 - Means 'following up on this matter' or 'revisiting.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 多寶 - Refers to a place of great value or a temple, possibly a Buddhist temple.
  • 爱公 - Refers to a person of respect, likely a monk or a respected individual.
  • 建元元年 - Refers to the first year of the Jianyuan era, a specific period in Chinese history.
  • 唐释道宣 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty monk Daoxuan, known for his works on Buddhist texts.
  • 三宝感通录 - A text compiled by Daoxuan, which records various miraculous events and their significance.
  • 津梁 - A metaphor for a bridge or a means of connection, often used in religious contexts to denote a spiritual link.
  • 夫镜接近情，莫踰仪像 - A philosophical statement suggesting that while a mirror reflects emotions, an image (likely a religious or artistic one) has a deeper emotional and spiritual impact.
  • 瑞验之发，多自此兴 - A statement indicating that many miraculous signs and events originate from the image or the place where the image is found.

Proper Nouns

  • 多寶 - A place of great value or a temple.
  • 爱公 - A respected individual, likely a monk.
  • 建元元年 - The first year of the Jianyuan era.
  • 唐释道宣 - The Tang Dynasty monk Daoxuan.
  • 三宝感通录 - A text compiled by Daoxuan.
  • 津梁 - A metaphor for a bridge or a means of connection.
  • 夫镜接近情，莫逾仪像 - A philosophical statement.
  • 瑞验之发，多自此兴 - A statement about miraculous signs.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 經云 - Classical phrase meaning 'according to the scriptures' or 'as the scriptures say.'
  • 镕斲图缋类形相者 - Compound phrase: '镕斲' (róng zhuó) refers to the process of refining and shaping metal; '图缋' (tú huì) refers to painting or illustration; '类形相者' (lèi xíng xiàng zhě) refers to those who create images or representations.
  • 爰能行动 - Means 'thus they can move' or 'therefore they can act.'
  • 及放光明 - Means 'and emit light' or 'and shine forth.'
  • 晖用若冥 - Means 'the light is as if in darkness' or 'the light is dim.'
  • 一引作真 - Variant reading that suggests 'one interpretation makes it true.'
  • 真楷作模 - Means 'true model' or 'authentic example.'
  • 神应亟着 - Means 'divine responses are often recorded' or 'divine manifestations are frequently documented.'
  • 因会所感 - Means 'due to the occasion or event, they are moved.'
  • 假冯木石 - Means 'relying on wood and stone' or 'using wood and stone as a medium.'
  • 以见幽异 - Means 'to reveal the mysterious and extraordinary.'
  • 沈石浮深 - Refers to a story where a stone sinks and then floats, symbolizing a transformation or revelation.
  • 尘金泻液 - Refers to a story where dust and gold are mixed, symbolizing a miraculous event.
  • 铨示繁方 - Means 'various methods of illustration' or 'multiple ways of representation.'
  • 允归自从 - Means 'truly return to their own nature' or 'truly follow their own path.'
  • 经塔显效 - Refers to the effectiveness or miracles associated with Buddhist stupas.
  • 旨证亦同 - Means 'the intent and evidence are the same.'
  • 事非殊贯 - Means 'the events are not different in essence.'
  • 故继其末 - Means 'therefore, following it up.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 释迦弥勒 - Refers to the historical Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama) and the future Buddha (Maitreya).
  • 西域 - Refers to the Western Regions, which historically included areas west of China, including parts of Central Asia and India.
  • 华夏 - Refers to the Chinese civilization or the central plains of China.
  • 景楷 - Refers to a type of Buddhist statue or image.
  • 法苑珠林 - A large compendium of Buddhist stories and teachings compiled during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 感通录 - A collection of stories about spiritual connections and divine interventions.

Proper Nouns

  • 释迦 - Shakyamuni (Siddhartha Gautama), the historical Buddha.
  • 弥勒 - Maitreya, the future Buddha.
  • 法苑珠林 - A large compendium of Buddhist stories and teachings compiled during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 感通录 - A collection of stories about spiritual connections and divine interventions.
  • 闽吴 - Regions in southeastern China.
  • 彭宋 - Regions in central China.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 形垂二丈 - Describes the figure as being two zhang in height, emphasizing its imposing stature.
  • 身黄金色 - Describes the figure's body as golden in color.
  • 项佩日光 - Describes the figure wearing a sunbeam as a pendant around the neck.
  • 得无是乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Is it not this?' or 'Could it be this?'
  • 写致经像 - Means 'to write and bring the scriptures and images.'
  • 表之中夏 - Means 'to present in the Central Plains' or 'to spread in the Central Plains.'
  • 咸敬事之 - Means 'all revere and serve him/her.'
  • 惧然自失 - Means 'feeling fear and losing one's composure.'
  • 乃遣画工图之数本 - Means 'then sent painters to draw several copies.'
  • 于南宫清凉台及高阳门显节寿陵上供养 - Means 'to place them on the Qingliang Terrace in the Nan Gong, the Gaoyang Gate, and the Xianjie Shouling.'
  • 如诸传备载 - Means 'as recorded in various biographies.'
  • 法苑珠林十三 - Refers to the *Fa Yuan Zhu Lin* (Jewel Forest of the Dharma Garden), a collection of Buddhist stories, specifically the 13th volume.
  • 三宝感通录二 - Refers to the *San Bao Gan Tong Lu* (Record of the Three Treasures' Responsive Communication), specifically the second volume.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉明帝 - Refers to Emperor Ming of Han, the fourth emperor of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 天竺 - Refers to ancient India, the birthplace of Buddhism.
  • 蔡愔 - A Chinese envoy sent by Emperor Ming to India to retrieve Buddhist scriptures and images.
  • 西域沙门迦叶摩腾 - Refers to the Indian monk Kasyapa Matanga, one of the first Buddhist monks to arrive in China.
  • 优填王画释迦佛像 - Refers to the statue of the Buddha created by King Udayana of Gandhara, which was brought to China.
  • 南宫清凉台 - A terrace in the Nan Gong (Southern Palace) where the Buddha images were placed.
  • 高阳门 - A gate in the imperial city of Luoyang.
  • 显节寿陵 - A tomb in the imperial cemetery.
  • 白马寺 - The first Buddhist temple in China, built in the capital city of Luoyang.
  • 法苑珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and anecdotes compiled during the Tang dynasty.
  • 三宝感通录 - A record of miraculous events involving the Three Treasures (Buddha, Dharma, Sangha).

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尝令乳母取先所弄指环 - Once asked his wet nurse to fetch the finger ring he had played with before.
  • 吾生三日时，头首北户，觉风吹顶 - When I was three days old, my head was at the northern door, and I felt the wind blowing on my head.
  • 病源既久，不可治也 - The disease has been long-standing, it cannot be cured.
  • 前身承有诸罪，赖造此寺，故获申济 - In my previous life, I committed various sins, thanks to the construction of this temple, I was thus able to be saved.
  • 所以使供养之情偏殷勤重也 - Which is why my devotion to offerings is particularly diligent and sincere.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太傅 - A high-ranking official position, often held by individuals with significant influence and respect.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains in China, often associated with longevity and immortality.
  • 武当寺 - Wudang Temple, a significant Buddhist temple in China, often associated with spiritual and religious practices.
  • 因果 - The concept of cause and effect, a fundamental principle in Buddhism.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋羊太傅祜 - Yang Hu, a high-ranking official during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai, a sacred mountain in China.
  • 武当寺 - Wudang Temple, a significant Buddhist temple in China.
  • 法苑珠林 - A Buddhist text that compiles various stories and teachings.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋沙门仕行慧皎高僧传作士行者 - Introduces the subject, 'Jin monk Shixing (also known as Shihang in the *Gao Seng Zhuan*), indicating he is a monk from the Jin dynasty, and his name is recorded differently in the *Gao Seng Zhuan*.
  • 气志方远，识宇沈正 - Describes Shixing's character: 'with a lofty spirit and far-reaching aspirations, and a deep and upright understanding.'
  • 循心直诣，荣辱不能动焉 - Means 'following his heart directly, he was not moved by honor or disgrace.'
  • 经典未备，唯有小品 - Indicates that the Buddhist scriptures were incomplete, with only the shorter versions available.
  • 章句阙略，义致弗显 - Means 'the verses and sentences were incomplete, and the meaning was not clearly expressed.'
  • 发迹雍州，西至于阗 - Describes Shixing's journey: 'he set out from Yongzhou and traveled west to Yutian (ancient name for Khotan).'
  • 踰历诸国 - Means 'he passed through various countries.'
  • 小乘学 - Refers to 'Hinayana Buddhism,' which is contrasted with Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 边人不识正法，将多惑乱 - Means 'people on the border do not know the true Dharma, and they will be easily confused.'
  • 千载将末，法当东流 - Refers to a prophecy that the Dharma will spread to the East in the latter days.
  • 若疑非佛说，请以至诚验之 - Means 'if you doubt that it is the Buddha's teaching, please test it with utmost sincerity.'
  • 焚柴灌油 - Describes the act of burning wood and pouring oil, likely as a form of ritual or test.
  • 烟炎方盛，仕行捧经涕泪 - Means 'as the smoke and flames were at their peak, Shixing held the scriptures and wept.'
  • 稽颡誓曰 - Means 'he knelt and swore, saying.'
  • 若果出金口，应宣布汉地 - Means 'if it truly came from the Buddha's mouth, it should be announced in Han territory.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋 - Refers to the Jin dynasty (265–420 CE), a period in Chinese history.
  • 雍州 - Refers to Yongzhou, a region in ancient China.
  • 于阗 - Refers to Yutian, an ancient kingdom in what is now Xinjiang, China.
  • 小乘学 - Refers to Hinayana Buddhism, which emphasizes individual liberation.
  • 正法 - Refers to the true or correct Dharma, the teachings of the Buddha.
  • 千载将末，法当东流 - Refers to a prophecy in Buddhist scriptures that the Dharma will spread to the East in the latter days.
  • 金口 - A term used to refer to the Buddha's teachings, implying that they are as valuable as gold.
  • 汉地 - Refers to the Han territories, indicating China.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋 - The Jin dynasty (265–420 CE).
  • 雍州 - Yongzhou, an ancient region in China.
  • 于阗 - Yutian, an ancient kingdom in what is now Xinjiang, China.
  • 小乘学 - Hinayana Buddhism.
  • 正法 - The true Dharma, the teachings of the Buddha.
  • 金口 - A term used to refer to the Buddha's teachings.
  • 汉地 - The Han territories, indicating China.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 諸佛菩薩 - Refers to the Buddhas and Bodhisattvas, the enlightened beings in Buddhism.
  • 宜為證明 - Means 'should serve as witnesses' or 'should certify,' indicating a formal endorsement or attestation.
  • 投經火中 - Literally 'throwing the scripture into the fire,' a ritual act of burning scriptures, often symbolic of purification or destruction.
  • 騰燎移景 - Describes the rising flames and the movement of shadows, likely a poetic way to describe the burning process.
  • 一積煨盡 - Means 'all the ashes have burned away,' indicating the complete consumption of the material.
  • 文字無毀 - Means 'the characters remained undamaged,' suggesting that the script or text was preserved despite the fire.
  • 皮牒若故 - Means 'the parchment or document seemed as if it were still new,' indicating that the physical appearance of the document was unchanged.
  • 舉國欣敬 - Means 'the entire country rejoiced and respected,' indicating widespread approval and reverence.
  • 因留供养 - Means 'therefore, they were kept for worship,' indicating the decision to preserve the document for religious purposes.
  • 遣弟子法饶 - Means 'sent a disciple named Fa Rao,' indicating the sending of a specific individual to carry out a task.
  • 赍送梵本 - Means 'to carry and deliver the Sanskrit version,' indicating the transportation of a religious text.
  • 還至陈留浚仪仓垣诗寺 - Means 'returned to the Cangyuan Temple in Junyi, Chenliu,' indicating the destination of the text.
  • 高僧传云 - Means 'as recorded in the Biography of Eminent Monks,' indicating a historical source.
  • 陈留仓垣水南寺出之 - Means 'as recorded by the Cangyuan Temple in Chenliu,' indicating the origin of the text.
  • 凡九十篇，二十万言 - Means 'totaling ninety chapters and two hundred thousand words,' indicating the size and scope of the text.
  • 河南居士竺叔兰 - Means 'the lay Buddhist scholar Zhubolan from Henan,' indicating the translator.
  • 练解方俗 - Means 'well-versed in local customs and dialects,' indicating Zhubolan's expertise.
  • 深善法味 - Means 'deeply appreciates the flavor of the Dharma,' indicating a profound understanding of Buddhist teachings.
  • 亲共传译 - Means 'personally involved in the translation,' indicating Zhubolan's active role in the translation process.
  • 今放光首品是也 - Means 'this is the first chapter of the *Flower Adornment Sutra*,' indicating the specific text being discussed.
  • 仕行八十乃亡 - Means 'served for eighty years before passing away,' indicating the length of Zhubolan's career.
  • 依阇维之火灭 - Means 'by the fire of cremation,' indicating the method of Zhubolan's death.
  • 经日尸形犹全 - Means 'after several days, the body remained intact,' indicating the unusual preservation of the body.
  • 国人惊异 - Means 'the people were surprised and amazed,' indicating the reaction of the community.
  • 若真得道法 - Means 'if truly attained the Dharma,' indicating a condition for the body's preservation.
  • 应声碎散 - Means 'it would break apart at the sound,' indicating the expected outcome if the body were not truly enlightened.
  • 乃敛骨起塔 - Means 'they then collected the bones and built a stupa,' indicating the posthumous treatment of Zhubolan's remains.
  • 慧志道人先师相传 - Means 'as recorded by the monk Huizhi, who inherited the teachings from his predecessors,' indicating a historical source.
  • 释公亦具载其事也 - Means 'Master Shi also recorded this event,' indicating another historical source.
  • 法苑珠林二十八 - Means 'Chapter 28 of the *Jewels of the Dharma Garden*,' indicating the source of the passage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 诸佛菩萨 - Refers to the enlightened beings in Buddhism, central figures in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 投经火中 - A ritual act of burning scriptures, often symbolic of purification or destruction, common in Buddhist tradition.
  • 腾燎移景 - Describes the rising flames and the movement of shadows, likely a poetic way to describe the burning process, reflecting the beauty and solemnity of the ritual.
  • 举国欣敬 - Indicates widespread approval and reverence, reflecting the importance of the event in the community.
  • 因留供养 - Indicates the decision to preserve the document for religious purposes, reflecting the value placed on sacred texts.
  • 遣弟子法饶 - Indicates the sending of a specific individual to carry out a task, reflecting the organizational structure of Buddhist monasteries.
  • 赍送梵本 - Indicates the transportation of a religious text, reflecting the importance of preserving and disseminating sacred texts.
  • 陈留仓垣水南寺出之 - Indicates the origin of the text, reflecting the historical and geographical context of the translation.
  • 河南居士竺叔兰 - Indicates the translator, reflecting the role of lay scholars in the transmission of Buddhist texts.
  • 练解方俗 - Indicates Zhubolan's expertise in local customs and dialects, reflecting the importance of cultural adaptation in translation.
  • 深善法味 - Indicates a profound understanding of Buddhist teachings, reflecting the translator's deep engagement with the Dharma.
  • 亲共传译 - Indicates Zhubolan's active role in the translation process, reflecting the collaborative nature of translation in Buddhism.
  • 今放光首品是也 - Indicates the specific text being discussed, reflecting the importance of the *Flower Adornment Sutra* in Buddhist tradition.
  • 仕行八十乃亡 - Indicates the length of Zhubolan's career, reflecting the dedication and longevity of his service.
  • 依阇维之火灭 - Indicates the method of Zhubolan's death, reflecting the Buddhist practice of cremation.
  • 经日尸形犹全 - Indicates the unusual preservation of the body, reflecting the belief in the sanctity of the body of enlightened beings.
  • 国人惊异 - Indicates the reaction of the community, reflecting the awe and respect accorded to enlightened beings.
  • 若真得道法 - Indicates a condition for the body's preservation, reflecting the belief in the supernatural powers of enlightened beings.
  • 应声碎散 - Indicates the expected outcome if the body were not truly enlightened, reflecting the belief in the miraculous nature of enlightened beings.
  • 乃敛骨起塔 - Indicates the posthumous treatment of Zhubolan's remains, reflecting the Buddhist practice of building stupas to honor the dead.

  • 如此，数人碎烂 - Like this, several people were crushed to pieces, where '碎烂' (suì làn) means 'crushed to pieces'.
  • 吏呼衡道人来当轮立 - The clerk called out for Zhi Faheng to stand before the wheel, where '道人' (dào rén) means 'monk'.
  • 衡恐怖自责，悔不精进 - Zhi Faheng was terrified and blamed himself, regretting not having been diligent, where '恐怖' (kǒngbù) means 'terrified', '自责' (zìzé) means 'blamed himself', and '悔不精进' (huǐ bù jīngjìn) means 'regretting not having been diligent'.
  • 今当此轮乎 - Will I be hit by this wheel now?, where '今当此轮乎' (jīn dāng cǐ lún hū) is a rhetorical question.
  • 道人可去 - Monk, you can go, where '道人可去' (dào rén kě qù) means 'monk, you can go'.
  • 仰首，见天有孔，不觉倏尔上升 - He looked up and saw a hole in the sky, and suddenly he ascended, where '仰首' (yǎng shǒu) means 'looked up', '见天有孔' (jiàn tiān yǒu kǒng) means 'saw a hole in the sky', and '倏尔上升' (shū'ěr shàngshēng) means 'suddenly ascended'.
  • 以头穿中，两手搏两边，四向顾视 - He put his head through the hole, held onto both sides with his hands, and looked around in all directions, where '以头穿中' (yǐ tóu chuān zhōng) means 'put his head through the hole', '两手搏两边' (liǎng shǒu bó liǎng biān) means 'held onto both sides with his hands', and '四向顾视' (sì xiàng gù shì) means 'looked around in all directions'.
  • 见七宝宫殿，及诸天人 - He saw palaces made of seven treasures and various heavenly beings, where '七宝宫殿' (qībǎo gōngdiàn) means 'palaces made of seven treasures' and '诸天人' (zhū tiān rén) means 'various heavenly beings'.
  • 衡甚踊跃，不能得上 - Zhi Faheng was very excited but could not ascend, where '踊跃' (yuèyǒng) means 'excited' or 'enthusiastic'.
  • 疲而复还下所 - He grew tired and returned to the lower place, where '疲而复还下所' (pí ér fù huán xià suǒ) means 'grew tired and returned to the lower place'.
  • 将衡去 - They took Zhi Faheng away, where '将衡去' (jiāng héng qù) means 'took Zhi Faheng away'.
  • 人笑曰 - People laughed and said, where '人笑曰' (rén xiào yuē) means 'people laughed and said'.
  • 见何等物，不能上乎 - What did you see that you couldn't ascend?, where '见何等物，不能上乎' (jiàn hé děng wù, bù néng shàng hū) is a rhetorical question.
  • 乃以衡付船官 - Then they handed Zhi Faheng over to the ship officials, where '乃以衡付船官' (nǎi yǐ héng fù chuán guān) means 'then they handed Zhi Faheng over to the ship officials'.
  • 船官行船，使为柂工 - The ship officials were operating the ship and made him the helmsman, where '船官行船' (chuán guān xíng chuán) means 'ship officials were operating the ship' and '使为柂工' (shǐ wéi yǐ gōng) means 'made him the helmsman'.
  • 我不能持柂 - I cannot hold the tiller, where '我不能持柂' (wǒ bù néng chí yǐ) means 'I cannot hold the tiller'.
  • 强之 - Forced him, where '强之' (qiǎng zhī) means 'forced him'.
  • 有船数百，皆随衡后 - There were hundreds of ships following behind him, where '有船数百' (yǒu chuán shùbǎi) means 'there were hundreds of ships' and '皆随衡后' (jiē suí héng hòu) means 'following behind him'.
  • 衡不晓捉柂，跄沙洲上 - Zhi Faheng did not know how to hold the tiller and crashed onto a sandbar, where '不晓捉柂' (bù xiǎo zhuō yǐ) means 'did not know how to hold the tiller' and '跄沙洲上' (qiāng shāzhōu shàng) means 'crashed onto a sandbar'.
  • 吏司推衡，汝道而失，以法应斩 - The officials pushed Zhi Faheng and said, 'You led the ship astray, and according to the law, you should be executed', where '吏司推衡' (lì sī tuī héng) means 'officials pushed Zhi Faheng', '汝道而失' (rǔ dào ér shī) means 'you led the ship astray', and '以法应斩' (yǐ fǎ yìng zhǎn) means 'according to the law, you should be executed'.

  • 不知何国人 - Common expression used to describe people of unknown origin in classical texts.

  • 尚纯朴，不表辞饰 - Still pure and simple, not showing eloquent or ornate words.
  • 时咸以此重之 - At that time, all esteemed him for this.
  • 异日 - One day or on another day.
  • 调忽往其家 - Fotiao suddenly went to his house.
  • 弟具问嫂所苦，并审兄安否 - The younger brother fully inquired about his sister-in-law’s suffering and examined his brother’s well-being.
  • 调去后，弟亦策马继往，言及调旦来 - After Fotiao left, the younger brother also rode his horse to follow, and mentioned that Fotiao had come that morning.
  • 兄弟争问调，调笑而不答，咸共异焉 - The brothers both asked Fotiao, but he smiled and did not answer, and all were astonished.
  • 调或独入深山，一年半岁，赍干饭数升，还恒有余 - Fotiao sometimes went alone into the deep mountains, for a year or half a year, carrying a few measures of dry rice, and upon returning, there was always some left over.
  • 有人尝随调山行数十里 - Someone once followed Fotiao on a mountain journey for dozens of miles.
  • 天?大雪，调入石穴虎窟中宿 - On a day with heavy snow, Fotiao entered a stone cave or tiger's den to stay the night. '天?' is a character that appears to be missing or corrupted. It may be '天' (heaven) or '天降大雪' (heavy snow fell from heaven).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋 - Refers to the Jin dynasty (265–420 CE), a period in Chinese history known for its political fragmentation and cultural flourishing.
  • 沙门 - A term for Buddhist monks or ascetics, often used in Chinese texts to refer to members of the Buddhist clergy.
  • 常山 - A place name, likely referring to Changshan (常山), a region in present-day Hebei Province, China. It was a significant location during the Jin dynasty.
  • 奉法者 - Those who follow the law or devotees of the Dharma – refers to individuals who practice or follow Buddhist teachings.
  • 和尚 - A term for 'monk' or 'priest,' used here to refer to Fotiao.
  • 干饭数升 - A few measures of dry rice – a reference to simple, monastic sustenance, indicating ascetic practices.
  • 石穴虎窟 - Stone cave or tiger's den – natural or dangerous places, suggesting Fotiao’s ability to survive in the wild, possibly indicating supernatural or superhuman abilities.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋 - The Jin dynasty (265–420 CE).
  • 佛调 - Fotiao, the name of the monk. This is likely a transliteration of a Sanskrit or Pali name, possibly 'Bhadra' or similar.
  • 常山 - A place name, likely referring to Changshan, a region in present-day Hebei Province.

  • 合堂惊跃，皆得睹见 - Everyone in the hall was shocked and jumped up, all able to see him.

  • 余经数台 - Several other scriptures. The word '台' (tai) is unusual in this context and may be a variant of '帙' (zhi), meaning 'scroll' or 'volume.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 晋董吉者，于潜人也 - This opening clause introduces the subject and his place of origin, but it is elliptical and requires some interpretation to clarify the relationship between the subject and the place.
  • 奉法三世，至吉尤精进 - This clause describes the subject's long-standing tradition of practicing the Dharma, with a particular emphasis on his dedication. The phrase '至吉尤精进' can be read as 'especially devoted to Ji,' but it is unclear whether '至吉' refers to Ji himself or the tradition up to Ji.
  • 恒斋戒诵首楞严经 - This clause describes Ji's daily practice, but the exact nature of the practice (e.g., whether he recites the sutra continuously or intermittently) is not specified.
  • 屯中有病，辄请吉读经 - This clause describes the villagers' habit of inviting Ji to read the sutras when someone is sick, but the exact timing and frequency of these invitations are not specified.
  • 所救多愈 - This clause states that most of those Ji helped recovered, but it is unclear whether this refers to physical recovery or spiritual liberation.
  • 亦奉法士也 - This clause introduces He as another practitioner of the Dharma, but the exact nature of his practice is not specified.
  • 咸和中，卒得山毒之病，守困 - This clause describes He's sudden contraction of a poisonous mountain illness and his subsequent severe illness and bedridden state.

  • 当衢而立 - Standing in the middle of the road may symbolize a central role or a pivotal moment.
  • 指语两人 - A gesture or command, possibly guiding the monk on his journey.
  • 将荷西行 - Taking the monk to the west, which may symbolize a journey toward enlightenment.
  • 屋舍甚多 - Many houses may symbolize a bustling or populated spiritual realm.
  • 白壁赤柱 - White walls and red columns are common in traditional Chinese architecture, symbolizing purity and strength.
  • 入一家 - Entering a house may symbolize entering a new phase or realm.
  • 美容服 - Beautifully dressed may symbolize a state of grace or purity.
  • 就乞食 - Asking for food may symbolize a humble or needy state.
  • 空中声言 - A voice from the air may symbolize divine guidance or communication.
  • 勿与之也 - Do not give it to him, may be a test of the monk's virtue or a command from a higher power.
  • 有人从地踊出 - Emerging from the ground may symbolize a supernatural or divine appearance.
  • 执铁杵 - An iron pestle may symbolize a tool of transformation or a weapon.

  • 历入十许家 - Entering ten or so houses may symbolize a series of trials or experiences.
  • 遂无所得 - Finally getting nothing may symbolize a lack of material gain or a need for spiritual growth.
  • 见一妪乘车 - Seeing an old woman riding in a carriage may symbolize a wise or guiding figure.
  • 与荷一卷书 - Giving the monk a scroll may symbolize a gift or message from a higher power.
  • 西至一家 - Reached a house to the west, which may symbolize a journey toward enlightenment.
  • 馆宇华整 - Elegant and well-ordered buildings may symbolize a well-ordered spiritual realm.
  • 坐于户外 - Sitting outside the house may symbolize a state of openness or exposure.
  • 口中虎牙 - Having tiger teeth in her mouth may symbolize a supernatural or mythical feature.
  • 床帐光丽 - Beautifully decorated beds and curtains may symbolize a luxurious or sacred space.
  • 竹席青几 - Bamboo mat and green table may symbolize a simple or natural setting.
  • 复有女子处之 - There was another woman there, which may symbolize a guide or a helper.
  • 荷以书卷与之 - Huìdá gave the scroll to her, which may symbolize a transfer of knowledge or a gift.
  • 女取余书比之 - The woman took the remaining books and compared them, which may symbolize a review or a comparison of knowledge.
  • 俄见两沙门 - Soon saw two monks, which may symbolize a meeting with spiritual guides.
  • 今宜归命释迦文佛 - Now it is appropriate to devote oneself to the Buddha Śākyamuni, which may symbolize a call to spiritual devotion.
  • 荷如言发念 - Huìdá followed the words and made a vow, which may symbolize a commitment to spiritual practice.

  • 遥见一城 - Saw a city in the distance, which may symbolize a vision of a spiritual realm.

  • 盖铁城也 - It was a city of iron, which may symbolize a strong or unyielding spiritual realm.
  • 见人身甚长大肤黑如漆 - Saw people with very tall and large bodies, their skin black like lacquer, which may symbolize a description of a supernatural or divine being.
  • 头发曳地 - Hair dragging on the ground, which may symbolize a state of humility or submission.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沙門曰 - Formal expression meaning 'the monk said.'
  • 此獄中鬼也 - Statement identifying the entity as a ghost from the underworld.
  • 其处甚寒 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the place is very cold.'
  • 有冰如席，飞散着人 - Vivid description of ice shards flying and hitting people.
  • 着头，头断；着脚，脚断 - Hyperbolic expression describing the severe consequences of being hit by the ice.
  • 二沙门云 - Formal expression meaning 'the two monks said.'
  • 此寒冰狱也 - Statement identifying the place as the Cold Ice Hell.
  • 荷便识宿命 - Phrase meaning 'the monk immediately recognized his past life.'
  • 知两沙门往维卫佛时，并其师也 - Statement indicating that the two monks were disciples of the same teacher during the time of the Buddha Vipasyin.
  • 作沙弥时，以犯俗罪，不得受戒 - Statement describing the monk's past as a novice who committed a lay sin and thus could not receive ordination.
  • 世虽有佛，竟不得见从 - Statement indicating that despite the presence of the Buddha, the monk never had the opportunity to follow him.
  • 再得人身：一生羌中，今生晋中 - Statement indicating that the monk has been reborn twice: once in Qiang and now in Jin.
  • 又见从伯，在此狱里 - Statement indicating that the monk sees his uncle in this hell.
  • 谓荷曰 - Formal expression meaning 'the uncle said to the monk.'
  • 昔在邺时，不知事佛 - Statement indicating that the uncle did not know about Buddhism in the past when he was in Ye.
  • 见人灌像，聊试学之 - Statement indicating that the uncle saw someone pouring water over a statue and tried it himself.
  • 而不肯还直 - Statement indicating that the uncle did not return the water.
  • 今故受罪 - Statement indicating that the uncle is now suffering punishment.
  • 犹有灌福，幸得生天 - Statement indicating that the uncle still has the merit from pouring water and will be reborn in heaven.
  • 次见刀山地狱 - Statement indicating that the monk next sees the Blade Mountain Hell.
  • 次第经历，观见甚多 - Statement indicating that the monk experiences various hells in sequence and sees many things.
  • 狱狱异城，不相杂厕 - Statement indicating that each hell is in a different city and does not mix with others.
  • 人数如沙，不可称计 - Statement indicating that the number of people in the hells is as numerous as sand and cannot be counted.
  • 楚毒科法，略与经说相符 - Statement indicating that the punishments and rules in the hells are roughly consistent with what is said in the scriptures.
  • 自荷履践地狱 - Statement indicating that the monk experiences the hells firsthand.
  • 示有光景 - Statement indicating that there is a glimmer of light.
  • 俄而忽见金色，晖明皎然 - Statement indicating that suddenly the monk sees a golden light that is bright and clear.
  • 见人长二丈许，相好严华，体黄金色 - Description of a person who is about two zhang tall, with a beautiful appearance and a golden body.
  • 左右并曰 - Formal expression meaning 'those around him said.'
  • 观世大士也 - Identification of the person as the Bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara.
  • 皆起迎礼 - Statement indicating that everyone rises to greet and pay respects.
  • 有二沙门，形质相类，并行而东 - Description of two monks who look similar and walk east together.
  • 菩萨具为说法，可千余言 - Statement indicating that the Bodhisattva gives a detailed explanation, speaking for more than a thousand words.
  • 凡为亡人设福，若父母兄弟，爰至七世，姻媾亲戚，朋友路人 - Statement indicating that setting up merit for the deceased, whether they are parents, siblings, or even friends and passersby, is beneficial.
  • 或在精舍，或在家中 - Statement indicating that the merit can be set up in a temple or at home.
  • 亡者受苦，即得免脱 - Statement indicating that the deceased can be freed from suffering.
  • 七月望日，沙门受腊 - Statement indicating that on the 15th day of the seventh month, monks receive their year-end offerings.
  • 此时设供，弥为胜也 - Statement indicating that setting up offerings at this time is especially meritorious.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks.
  • 维卫佛 - Refers to the Buddha Vipasyin, one of the twenty-four Buddhas in the past.
  • 邺 - Refers to Ye, an ancient city in China.
  • 羌 - Refers to Qiang, a region in ancient China.
  • 晋 - Refers to Jin, a dynasty in Chinese history.
  • 观世大士 - Refers to the Bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara, also known as Guanyin.
  • 七月望日 - Refers to the 15th day of the seventh month in the lunar calendar, a significant date in Buddhist practice.
  • 沙门受腊 - Refers to the ceremony where monks receive their year-end offerings.

Proper Nouns

  • 沙门 - Buddhist monks.
  • 维卫佛 - Buddha Vipasyin.
  • 邺 - Ancient city in China.
  • 羌 - Region in ancient China.
  • 晋 - Dynasty in Chinese history.
  • 观世大士 - Bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara.
  • 七月望日 - 15th day of the seventh month in the lunar calendar.
  • 沙门受腊 - Ceremony where monks receive their year-end offerings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 若制器物，以充供养 - Means 'if one makes objects to fulfill offerings.' Reflects the Buddhist practice of creating objects for religious offerings.
  • 器器摽题 - Literally 'each object is marked with a title.' Means that each object is labeled or inscribed with its purpose or dedication.
  • 言为某人亲奉上三宝 - Stating that it is personally offered by a certain person to the Triple Gem.
  • 福施弥多，其庆逾速 - 'The more merit is given, the faster the reward comes.' A common Buddhist expression linking generosity to karmic reward.
  • 沙门白衣 - 'Monks and laypeople.' *沙门* (śramaṇa) refers to Buddhist monks or ascetics, while *白衣* (white-clad) refers to laypeople.
  • 见身为过，及宿世之罪 - 'Seeing one's current body as a source of transgression, and the sins of past lives.' Reflects the Buddhist concept of karmic retribution.
  • 种种恶业 - 'Various evil deeds' or 'karmic offenses.'
  • 能于众中尽自发露 - 'Able to fully confess in public.' *发露* is a term used in Buddhist practice for public confession of sins.
  • 不失事条 - 'Without losing the order of events.' This suggests maintaining clarity or adherence to the proper sequence of confession.
  • 勤诚忏悔者 - 'Those who diligently and sincerely repent.'
  • 罪即消灭 - 'The sins are immediately destroyed or eliminated.'
  • 若有所遗漏，非故隐蔽 - 'If something is omitted, not intentionally hidden.' This implies unintentional negligence rather than deliberate concealment.
  • 虽不获免，受报稍轻 - 'Though not entirely exempt, the retribution is lighter.'
  • 若不能悔，无惭愧心 - 'If one cannot repent, and lacks a sense of shame or remorse.'
  • 此名执过不反 - 'This is called clinging to one's faults without returning (to the path).' *不反* suggests not turning back or not reforming.
  • 命终之后，克坠地狱 - 'After death, one will certainly fall into hell.' *克坠* is a strong expression meaning 'certainly fall.'
  • 他造塔及与堂殿 - 'If others build pagodas and halls.' *堂殿* refers to temple halls or structures.
  • 虽复一土一木，若染若碧 - 'Even if it is just one piece of earth or wood, whether dyed or green.' This refers to the smallest contributions, even if simple or modest.
  • 率诚供助 - 'Diligently and sincerely offer assistance.' *率诚* implies sincerity and earnestness.
  • 获福甚多 - 'Will gain great merit.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三宝 - A fundamental Buddhist concept referring to the Buddha (the enlightened one), the Dharma (the teachings), and the Sangha (the community of monks and nuns).
  • 沙门 - A term for Buddhist monks or ascetics, distinct from laypeople (*白衣*).
  • 忏悔 - A core Buddhist practice involving the acknowledgment and repentance of sins or wrongdoings, often in a public or communal setting.
  • 发露 - A specific form of confession in which a person publicly admits their faults, often in front of a monastic community.
  • 地狱 - A Buddhist concept of an afterlife realm where beings suffer as a result of negative karma.
  • 塔 - A structure used to house relics of the Buddha or important Buddhist figures.
  • 堂殿 - Refers to the buildings used in Buddhist monastic and religious practice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 见其家亡妹于后推之，踣尸面上，因得稣活 - Phrase meaning 'saw his deceased sister-in-law push him from behind, causing him to fall on the face of the corpse, and thus he revived.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宋陈安居者，襄阳县人也 - Formal introduction of a person, 'Cao Chen Anju, a native of Xiangyang County in the state of Song.'
  • 伯父少事巫俗，鼓舞祭祀，神影庙宇，充满其宅 - Describes the elder brother's involvement in shamanistic practices, including dancing and sacrificing, with temple images and structures filling his house.
  • 父独敬信释法，旦夕斋戒 - Indicates the father's solitary reverence and trust in Buddhist law, practicing daily fasting and purification.
  • 后伯父亡，无子，父以安居绍焉 - States that after the elder brother died without an heir, the father designated Anju as his successor.
  • 安居虽即伯舍，而理行精求，淫飨之事，废不复设 - Suggests that although Anju inherited the elder brother's house, he pursued a more refined and disciplined life, abandoning excessive feasting and sacrificial rituals.
  • 发则为歌神之曲，迷闷惛僻，如此者弥岁，而执心愈固 - Describes Anju's episodes where he would sing songs to the gods, becoming confused and dazed, persisting for a year, with his resolve growing stronger.
  • 若我不杀之志，遂当亏夺者，必先自脔截四体，乃就其事 - Anju's vow that if his determination to kill (possibly referring to himself) were to be thwarted, he would first cut off his limbs before proceeding.
  • 经积二年，永初元年，病发，遂绝，但心下微暖，家人不敛 - Indicates that after two years, in the first year of Yongchu, Anju fell ill, seemingly died, but with warmth still in his chest, so the family did not bury him.
  • 至七日夜，守视之者，觉尸足闲如有风来飘衣动衾，于是而稣有声，家人初惧尸蹶，并走避之，既而稍能转动，末求饮浆 - Describes the event where, on the seventh night, those watching noticed the corpse's feet moving as if wind was blowing, clothes and bedding fluttering, and then Anju made a sound of revival. Initially, the family feared the corpse was coming back to life and fled, but later, he began to move slightly and eventually asked for water.
  • 安居乃具说所经见云 - Anju then detailed what he had experienced.
  • 初有人若使者；将刀数十，呼将去 - Describes an initial encounter with someone resembling a messenger who carried many knives and called for Anju to follow.
  • 从者欲缚之，使者曰：“此人有福，未可缚也” - Indicates that followers wanted to bind him, but the messenger said, 'This person has good fortune, do not bind him.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 安居至合 - A proper name or title, possibly referring to a person or a place, but its exact meaning is unclear.
  • 钳梏者数百 - Refers to individuals who are shackled or restrained, and '数百' means 'hundreds.' Describes a large group of prisoners.
  • 一时俱进 - Means 'all advanced at once,' indicating that the prisoners were brought forward together.
  • 在第三 - Indicates the position or order, meaning 'in the third place.'
  • 既至阶下 - Means 'once they reached the steps below,' describing the location where the prisoners arrived.
  • 一人服冠冕 - Describes a person wearing a crown or hat, likely a judge or official.
  • 读诸罪簿 - Means 'read the records of various crimes,' referring to the official documentation of the prisoners' offenses.
  • 其第一者云 - Indicates the first entry in the records, '云' is an archaic particle used to introduce a statement.
  • 昔娶妻之始，夫妇为誓，有子无子，终不相弃 - This is a vow made by the couple at the time of their marriage, promising to stay together regardless of whether they had children or not.
  • 而其人本是祭酒，妻亦奉道，共化导徒众，得士女弟子，因而奸之，遂弃本妻 - The man was originally a *zhijiu* (a high-ranking official in a religious or scholarly organization), and his wife also followed the same path. They together led and educated many disciples, but he committed adultery and abandoned his original wife.
  • 妻常冤诉 - The wife frequently complained of her injustice.
  • 府君曰 - '府君' is a respectful term for a local official or judge.
  • 汝夫妇违誓大义，不罪二，终罪一也 - The judge says, 'You both violated the great principle of your vow, and you are not guilty of two crimes, but one.'

  • 忽见形诣女婿南阳刘凝之家 - This phrase means 'he suddenly appeared in the form of a ghost to visit his son-in-law Liu Ning's house in Nanyang.' '忽见形' means 'suddenly appeared in the form of a ghost,' and '诣' means 'to visit.'

  • 家当更有三人丧亡 - Means 'your family will have three more deaths.'
  • 邻家有道士祭酒，姓魏名叵 - Refers to a Taoist priest named Wei Po from a neighboring house.
  • 常为章符，诳化屯里 - Means 'often uses talismans and deceives the people in the garrison.'
  • 语龄曰 - Means 'told Liu Ling.'
  • 君家衰祸未已，由奉胡神故也 - Means 'Your family's misfortune has not ended because you worship foreign gods.'
  • 若事大道，必蒙福祐 - Means 'If you serve the great Tao, you will surely receive blessings.'
  • 不改意者，将来灭门 - Means 'If you do not change your mind, your entire family will be destroyed.'
  • 遂揭延祭酒，罢不奉法 - Means 'Liu Ling then invited the Taoist priest and stopped following the law.'
  • 宜焚去经像，灾乃当除耳 - Means 'You should burn the scriptures and statues, and the disaster will be eliminated.'
  • 像于中夜，又放火赫然 - Means 'In the middle of the night, the statue itself seemed to set fire again.'
  • 时诸祭酒有二十许人 - Means 'At that time, there were about twenty Taoist priests.'
  • 亦有惧畏灵验，密委去者 - Means 'Some were also afraid of the miraculous events and secretly left.'
  • 叵等师徒，犹盛意不止 - Means 'Wei Po and his disciples still persisted in their efforts.'
  • 被发偊步，执持刀索 - Means 'They went around with disheveled hair and weapons.'
  • 云斥佛还胡国，不得留中夏，为民害也 - Means 'They claimed to drive the Buddha back to the foreign land, not allowing him to stay in China, where he would harm the people.'
  • 龄于其夕，如有人殴打之者，顿仆于地，家人扶起，示余气息，遂委挛躄不能行动 - Means 'That night, it seemed as if someone was beating him. He fell to the ground, and his family helped him up, showing that he was still alive. He became paralyzed and could no longer move.'
  • 道士魏叵，其时体内发疽，日出二升，不过一月，受苦便死 - Means 'The Taoist priest Wei Po developed a boil inside his body. It produced two liters of pus daily, and he died within a month.'
  • 自外同伴，并皆着癞 - Means 'His fellow practitioners also contracted leprosy.'
  • 其邻人东安太守水丘和传于东阳无疑，时亦多有见者 - Means 'His neighbor, the governor of Dong'an, Shui Qiuhe, passed this story on to Dongyang Wuyi. Many people also saw these events.'
  • 珠林六十二 - Refers to the source of the story, 'Jade Forest, Volume 62.'

  • 道人曰：“彼二人自知罪福，知而故犯；唯尔愚蒙，不识缘报，故以相诫。” - Explains that the other two travelers knew their sins and rewards but deliberately committed them, while the third was ignorant and thus warned.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逐食 - Literally 'to seek food,' but in context, means 'to go out in search of food,' often implying a desperate situation.
  • 積漸 - Means 'gradually' or 'over time,' indicating a process of incremental accumulation.
  • 遂囊箧數四悉滿焉 - Describes the gradual filling of multiple bags or chests, emphasizing the quantity and completeness of the theft.
  • 因將 - Means 'therefore' or 'thus,' indicating a consequence.
  • 共铸為錢 - Means 'to melt down and cast into coins,' suggesting a collective effort.
  • 見人以火烧之 - '見人' can be interpreted as 'to see someone' or 'to be seen by someone.' '以火烧之' means 'to burn with fire,' which is likely a hallucination or a form of torture.
  • 楚毒不可堪忍 - '楚毒' refers to severe pain or torture, and '不可堪忍' means 'cannot be endured,' emphasizing the intensity of the suffering.
  • 未及刑坐而死 - '未及' means 'not yet' or 'before,' and '刑坐' refers to the act of being subjected to punishment. This phrase indicates that the person died before the official punishment was carried out.
  • 舉體皆炘裂 - '舉體' means 'the entire body,' and '炘裂' means 'to burst or split open,' suggesting a violent and painful death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宋費崇先者，吳興人也 - A formal introduction meaning 'Mr. Fei Chongxian of Song, from Wu County.'
  • 少颇信法 - Means 'in his youth, he had some faith in the Dharma (Buddhist teachings).'
  • 至三十际，精勤弥至 - Means 'by the age of thirty, his diligence and devotion became even more intense.'
  • 泰始三年，受菩萨戒 - Means 'in the third year of Tai Shi, he received the Bodhisattva precepts.'
  • 寄斋于谢惠远家 - Means 'he stayed for meditation at the home of Xie Huaiyuan.'
  • 二十四日，昼夜不懈 - Means 'for twenty-four days, without rest day or night.'
  • 每听经，常以鹊尾香炉置膝前 - Means 'every time he listened to the scriptures, he would place a magpie-tail incense burner on his knees.'
  • 初学记二十五引云 - Means 'according to the *Initial Learning Record* (卷二十五).'
  • 初斋三夕，见一人容服不凡 - Means 'on the third night of his initial meditation retreat, he saw a person of extraordinary appearance and attire.'
  • 迳来举炉将去 - Means 'who came straight to take the incense burner away.'
  • 崇先视膝前炉犹在其处 - Means 'Chongxian looked at the incense burner on his knees and found it still there.'
  • 更详视此人，见提去甚分明 - Means 'he looked more closely at the person and saw them take the incense burner away clearly.'
  • 崇先方悟是神异 - Means 'Chongxian then realized it was a supernatural phenomenon.'
  • 自惟衣裳新濯，了无不净 - Means 'he thought to himself that his clothes had just been washed and were completely clean.'
  • 唯坐侧有唾壶 - Means 'the only thing near him was a spittoon.'
  • 既使去壶，即复见此人还炉坐前 - Means 'after he removed the spittoon, he saw the person return with the incense burner to sit in front of him.'
  • 未至席顷犹见两炉，既即合为一 - Means 'for a short while he still saw two incense burners, but then they merged into one.'
  • 然则此神人所提者，盖炉影乎 - Means 'thus, what the divine person took away was probably just a shadow of the incense burner.'
  • 崇先又当闻人说 - Means 'Chongxian also heard from others that...'
  • 福远寺有僧钦尼精勤得道 - Means 'there was a nun named Qinni at Fuyuan Temple who was diligent and had attained enlightenment.'
  • 欣然愿见，未及得往，属意甚至 - Means 'he was delighted to see her, but before he could go, he had already developed a strong attachment.'
  • 尝斋于他家，夜三更中 - Means 'once, while staying for meditation at another's home, in the middle of the third watch of the night...'
  • 忽见一尼，容仪端严着赭布袈娑 - Means 'he suddenly saw a nun with dignified appearance, wearing a dark red robe.'
  • 正立斋席之前，食顷而灭 - Means 'standing upright in front of the meditation hall, she vanished after a meal's worth of time.'
  • 及崇先后觐此尼，色貌被服，即窗前所睹者也 - Means 'when Chongxian later met this nun, her appearance and attire were exactly as he had seen from the window.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 少而奉佛，至泰始中，随湘州刺史刘韬监营浦县 - This sentence introduces He Jingshu and his role, but the connection between his devotion to Buddhism and his official position is not immediately clear.
  • 敬叔时遇有旃檀，制以为像，像将就而未有光材 - This sequence describes the process of making a statue, but the transition between having the sandalwood and the need for the halo material is somewhat abrupt.
  • 敬叔意愿甚勤，而营索无处，凭几微睡，见一沙门纳衣杖锡来 - This part is a bit elliptical, but it describes He Jingshu's diligent search and his subsequent dream.
  • 上五字依广记引补语 - This phrase indicates that additional information is provided based on a historical record, which adds complexity to the narrative.
  • 鼠啮吾足，清旦疾归视像果然矣 - This final part is a bit disjointed, but it describes the statue's prediction and the subsequent verification.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今无复余物 - Literally means 'now there is nothing left,' indicating a state of poverty or scarcity.
  • 唯衣被毡履 - Refers to basic necessities: clothes, bedding, and shoes.
  • 或足充一会 - Means 'perhaps enough to sustain a single gathering' or 'just enough for one occasion.'
  • 并烦请愿具为，忏悔 - A formal request for others to perform a ritual of confession or repentance.
  • 昔偷像相珠有二枚 - Refers to a pair of stolen jewels (likely from a statue or image).
  • 一枚已属妪人 - Means 'one has already belonged to an old woman,' indicating the loss of one of the jewels.
  • 一以质钱，在陈照家 - Means 'the other was pawned for money and is now in the possession of Chen Zao.'
  • 今可赎取 - Means 'now it can be redeemed' or 'can be bought back.'
  • 道志既死 - Indicates the death of Dao Zhi, a monk or person mentioned in the text.
  • 诸僧合集赎得相珠 - Means 'the monks collectively gathered to redeem the jewels.'
  • 并设斋忏 - Refers to a ritual meal (斋) and confession (忏) performed by the monks.
  • 工人复相珠时，展转迥趣，终不安合 - Describes a physical phenomenon where the jewels, when reassembled, fail to fit properly.
  • 众僧复为礼拜烧香 - Means 'the monks then performed prayers and burned incense.'
  • 乃得着焉 - Means 'and finally, they were able to fit' or 'they were successfully reassembled.'
  • 年余而同学等于昏夜闲闻空中有语 - Describes a supernatural event where fellow students hear voices in the air during the night.
  • 详听即道志声也 - Means 'upon careful listening, it was the voice of Dao Zhi.'
  • 自说云：自死以来，备萦痛毒，方累年劫，未有出期 - Means 'he said: Since my death, I have been enduring pain and suffering, enduring countless years, with no end in sight.'
  • 赖蒙众僧，哀怜救护，赎像相珠，故于苦酷之中，时有闲息 - Means 'Thanks to the compassion and rescue of the monks, who redeemed the jewels, I occasionally find brief respite from my suffering.'
  • 感恩罔已，故暂来称谢，言此而已 - Means 'I am endlessly grateful, so I have come temporarily to express my thanks, saying only this.'

  • 形气伟壮 - Means 'physically imposing and strong.'
  • 谓之曰 - Means 'told him.'
  • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗 - '涅槃尊经' means 'the revered *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*,' and '众藏之宗' means 'the root of all collections.'
  • 何得以君璅思，轻加斟酌 - '何得' means 'how can,' '璅思' means 'trivial thoughts,' and '轻加斟酌' means 'lightly edit.'
  • 严怅然不释 - '怅然' means 'disappointed,' and '不释' means 'not let go.'
  • 犹以发意 - '犹以' means 'still because of,' and '发意' means 'intention.'
  • 苟觅多知 - '苟' means 'if,' '觅' means 'seek,' and '多知' means 'many knowledges.'
  • 明夕将卧 - '明夕' means 'next night,' and '将卧' means 'about to sleep.'
  • 复见昨人 - '复见' means 'saw again,' and '昨人' means 'the person from yesterday.'
  • 甚有怒色 - '甚有' means 'very much,' and '怒色' means 'angry expression.'
  • 过而知改，是谓非过 - '过而知改' means 'to recognize and correct mistakes,' and '是谓非过' means 'that is not a mistake.'
  • 昨故相告，犹不己乎 - '昨故相告' means 'yesterday I warned you,' and '犹不己乎' means 'still not stopped?'
  • 此经既无行理 - '既无行理' means 'has no reason to be practiced.'
  • 且君祸亦将及 - '且' means 'and,' '君祸' means 'your misfortune,' and '亦将及' means 'will also come to you.'
  • 严惊觉失措 - '惊觉' means 'startled awake,' and '失措' means 'at a loss.'
  • 未及申旦 - '未及' means 'before,' and '申旦' means 'dawn.'
  • 便驰信求还 - '便' means 'then,' '驰信' means 'send a message,' and '求还' means 'request to return.'
  • 悉烧除之 - '悉' means 'all,' and '烧除之' means 'burned and destroyed.'
  • 尘外精舍释道俨具所谙闻也 - '尘外精舍' means 'a hermitage outside the world,' '释道俨' is the name of a monk, and '具所谙闻' means 'has heard of it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗 - The *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* is considered a foundational text in Mahayana Buddhism.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 釋慧嚴京師東安寺僧也 - This phrase identifies Master Huaiyan as a monk at Dong'an Temple in the capital city.
  • 理思該暢，見器道俗 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's comprehensive and smooth reasoning and his esteem among both monastics and laypeople.
  • 大涅槃經文字繁多 - This clause states that the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* is verbose.
  • 遂加刊削，就成數卷，寫两三通，以示同好 - This sequence describes the editing process and the copying of the edited text for like-minded people.
  • 因寝寤之际，忽见一人，身长二丈余，形气伟壮，谓之曰 - This clause describes the monk's experience of seeing a tall and imposing figure in his dream.
  • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗，何得以君璅思，轻加斟酌 - This clause is the figure's warning about the importance of the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* and the inappropriateness of editing it.
  • 严怅然不释，犹以发意 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's disappointment and his continued intention to edit the text.
  • 苟觅多知明夕将卧，复见昨人，甚有怒色，谓曰 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's intention to seek more knowledge and his subsequent dream where the figure appears again with an angry expression.
  • 过而知改，是谓非过；昨故相告，犹不己乎 - This clause is the figure's admonition about recognizing and correcting mistakes.
  • 此经既无行理，且君祸亦将及 - This clause is the figure's warning about the consequences of editing the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
  • 严惊觉失措，未及申旦，便驰信求还，悉烧除之 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's reaction and his decision to burn the edited text.
  • 尘外精舍释道俨具所谙闻也 - This clause mentions that the monk from the hermitage has heard about the incident.

Proper Nouns

  • 釋慧嚴 - Master Huaiyan, a Buddhist monk.
  • 京師東安寺 - Dong'an Temple in the capital city.
  • 大涅槃經 - *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
  • 涅槃尊经 - The revered *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
  • 尘外精舍 - A hermitage outside the world.
  • 释道俨 - A monk who has heard about the incident.
  • 珠林十八 - The eighteenth chapter of the *Jade Forest* collection.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阖门遑惧，属纩待时 - The entire family was in great fear, waiting anxiously for her to pass away, describing the family's distress.
  • 费氏心念 - Fei thought to herself, introducing her inner monologue.
  • 我诵经勤苦，宜有善佑，庶不于此，遂致死也 - I have recited the scriptures diligently, I should be protected by good fortune, perhaps not here, leading to death, expressing her hope and reflection.
  • 既而睡卧，食顷，如寤如梦见佛于窗中，授手以摩其心，应时都愈 - Afterwards, she fell asleep, after a meal, as if waking up or dreaming, she saw a Buddha at the window, who placed his hand on her heart, and she was immediately cured, describing the miraculous event.
  • 一堂男女婢仆，悉睹金光，亦闻香气 - All the men, women, maids, and servants in the house saw golden light and smelled a fragrant aroma, detailing the shared experience.
  • 玙从妹即琰外族曾祖尚书中兵郎费愔之夫人也，于时省疾床前，亦具闻见 - Yu's cousin was the wife of Fei Yin, a former military official and great-grandson of Yu's uncle, at that time, she visited Fei at her bedside and also witnessed everything, describing her cousin's presence and observation.
  • 于是大兴信悟，虔戒至终，每以此瑞进化子侄焉 - Thus, she greatly strengthened her faith and enlightenment, remained devout until the end, and often used this miracle to inspire her nephews and nieces, summarizing the impact of the event.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沙门安法开者，北人也 - 沙门 refers to a Buddhist monk, 安法开 is the monk's name, and 北人也 indicates that he is from the north. This phrase introduces the subject of the passage.
  • 尝见吴公，长三尺，自屋堕地，旋回而去 - 尝见 means 'once saw,' 吴公 is a mysterious figure, 长三尺 describes his height, 自屋堕地 means 'fell from the house to the ground,' and 旋回而去 means 'turned around and left.' This describes an unusual encounter.
  • 御览九百四十六 - 御览 is a reference to the *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), a vast collection of stories and anecdotes, and 九百四十六 is the specific volume number.
  • 元嘉八年蒲阪城中大灾火 - 元嘉八年 is the year 431 AD during the Liu Song dynasty, 蒲阪 is a place name, and 大灾火 means 'a great fire disaster.' This sets the historical and geographical context.
  • 里中小屋虽焚，而于煨烬下得金经，纸素如故 - 里中 means 'in the village,' 小屋 means 'small houses,' 虽焚 means 'though burned,' 煨烬 means 'ashes,' 金经 means 'golden sutra,' and 纸素如故 means 'the paper remained as before.' This describes an unusual event during the fire.
  • 苏易简文房四谱四 - 苏易简 is the name of the author, 文房四谱 is the title of his work, and 四 refers to the fourth chapter or section.
  • 晋世有竺长舒者，本天竺人 - 晋世 refers to the Jin dynasty, 竺长舒 is the name of the person, and 本天竺人 means 'originally from India.' This introduces another subject.
  • 专心诵观世音经为业 - 专心 means 'diligently,' 诵 means 'recite,' 观世音经 is the *Guanyin Sutra*, and 为业 means 'as his profession.' This describes his practice.
  • 后居吴中 - 后 means 'later,' 居 means 'lived,' and 吴中 refers to the area around Suzhou. This indicates his relocation.
  • 邑内遭火，屋宇连栋，甍檐相接，火至皆焚，无能为救 - 邑内 means 'in the town,' 遭火 means 'suffered a fire,' 屋宇连栋 means 'houses connected in rows,' 甍檐相接 means 'roofs touching each other,' 火至皆焚 means 'the fire burned everything,' and 无能为救 means 'no one could stop it.' This describes the extent of the fire.
  • 长舒家正在下风，分意烧毁，一心唤观世音 - 下风 means 'downwind,' 分意烧毁 means 'thought it would be burned,' and 一心唤观世音 means 'called out to Guanyin with all his heart.' This describes his reaction.
  • 欲至舒家，风回火灭，竟家获免 - 欲至 means 'about to reach,' 风回 means 'the wind changed,' 火灭 means 'the fire was extinguished,' and 竟家获免 means 'his whole family was saved.' This describes the miraculous outcome.
  • 合县惊异，叹其有神 - 合县 means 'the whole county,' 惊异 means 'were amazed,' and 叹其有神 means 'admired his divine power.' This describes the reaction of the townspeople.
  • 时有凶恶少年，怪其老胡，有何灵应，火烧不然 - 时有 means 'there was,' 凶恶少年 means 'a wicked young man,' 怪其老胡 means 'wondered about the old foreigner,' 有何灵应 means 'what miraculous response,' and 火烧不然 means 'the fire didn't burn him.' This describes the skepticism of a young man.
  • 到后夜风急，少年以火投屋，四投皆灭，年少嗟感，至明，乃叩头首过 - 到后夜 means 'on the following night,' 风急 means 'the wind was strong,' 以火投屋 means 'threw fire at the house,' 四投皆灭 means 'all four attempts failed,' 年少嗟感 means 'the young man was moved,' 至明 means 'until dawn,' and 乃叩头首过 means 'then knelt and apologized.' This describes the young man's change of heart.
